title: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani
description: Indulge in the iconic Butter Chicken, a rich and creamy Indian curry featuring tender chicken pieces simmered in a velvety tomato-cashew gravy. Its harmonious blend of tangy, sweet, and subtly spicy flavors, finished with fresh cream and butter, makes it a beloved classic for a truly authentic Indian lunch.

ingredients.notes: finely chopped (optional, for a sweeter base)
or 5-6 medium ripe tomatoes, blanched, peeled, and pureed
soaked in warm water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with plain yogurt, 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tsp red chili powder, 1/2 tsp turmeric powder, 1 tsp garam masala, 1/2 tsp salt, and lemon juice. Mix thoroughly to ensure all chicken pieces are well coated. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours, up to overnight, for the best flavor and tenderness.

instructions.title: Cook the Marinated Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tbsp cooking oil in a large heavy-bottomed pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Remove the chicken from the marinade, shaking off any excess. Cook the chicken in batches, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan, until lightly browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes per batch. Set the cooked chicken aside on a plate.

instructions.title: Prepare the Gravy Base
instructions.text: In the same pan (or a clean pot), melt 2 tbsp ghee or unsalted butter with the remaining 1 tbsp cooking oil over medium heat. Add the whole spices: bay leaves, green cardamom pods, black cardamom pods (if using), cinnamon stick, and cloves. Sauté for about 30 seconds until fragrant. If using, add the finely chopped onion and cook until it turns soft and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Stir in 1 tbsp fresh ginger-garlic paste and the slit green chilies, cooking for another 1-2 minutes until aromatic.

instructions.title: Simmer and Blend the Gravy
instructions.text: Add the pureed tomatoes (or crushed canned tomatoes), pre-soaked cashews, crushed kasuri methi, 1 tsp red chili powder, 1/2 tsp turmeric powder, 1 tsp garam masala, sugar, and salt to the pot. Mix everything well. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, then cover and cook for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes have cooked down and you see oil beginning to separate at the edges. Remove the whole spices (bay leaf, cardamom pods, cinnamon stick) from the gravy. Let the gravy cool slightly, then carefully transfer it to a blender and blend until completely smooth. For an extra silky texture, pass the blended gravy through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pot.

instructions.title: Combine Chicken and Gravy
instructions.text: Return the smooth gravy to the pot over medium-low heat. Add the previously cooked chicken pieces and 1/2 cup of water or chicken stock. Stir gently to combine. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer and let it cook for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the rich flavors of the gravy.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Stir in the 1/2 cup fresh cream and the remaining 2 tbsp unsalted butter. Continue to cook for another 2-3 minutes, ensuring the gravy does not boil vigorously after adding the cream. Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary. Ladle the hot Butter Chicken into serving bowls. Garnish with a swirl of fresh cream and a sprinkle of chopped fresh coriander leaves. Serve immediately with warm naan, roti, or fragrant Basmati rice.

tips: For the most tender and flavorful chicken, marinate it for at least 2 hours, or even better, overnight in the refrigerator.
Blend the gravy until absolutely smooth, then strain it through a fine-mesh sieve. This extra step ensures the signature silky texture of authentic Butter Chicken.
Always use good quality, ripe red tomatoes or high-quality canned crushed tomatoes for the best flavor base for your gravy.
Add fresh cream and the finishing butter at the very end of cooking, and avoid boiling the gravy vigorously thereafter to prevent the cream from splitting and to preserve its delicate flavor.
article:
## History of Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani

Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, has a relatively recent but iconic history, originating in the bustling culinary landscape of Delhi. It was created in the 1950s by Kundan Lal Jaggi, Thakur Das Mago, and Kundan Lal Gujral at the Moti Mahal restaurant in Daryaganj, Delhi. The dish emerged from a need to utilize leftover Tandoori Chicken, which was often dry by the end of the day. The ingenious chefs simmered the chicken pieces in a rich, creamy, tomato-based gravy, adding butter and cream to create a succulent and flavorful dish. This innovation quickly captivated palates, leading Murgh Makhani to become one of India's most beloved and internationally recognized curries, embodying the rich culinary heritage of the Mughlai tradition with a modern twist.

## Why This Recipe Works

This recipe for Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani achieves its legendary status through a masterful balance of flavors and textures. The chicken is first marinated in yogurt and spices, then pan-fried or grilled to impart a slight smokiness and tender texture, preventing it from becoming bland. The heart of the dish lies in its velvety smooth gravy, a harmonious blend of ripe tomatoes, cashews for richness, and aromatic whole spices. The tang of tomatoes, the sweetness of a touch of sugar, and the warmth of garam masala are perfectly counterbalanced by the luxurious addition of fresh cream and generous amounts of butter. This interplay creates a complex yet comforting profile that is both deeply satisfying and incredibly addictive, making every bite a delightful experience.

## Chef Tips for Perfection

For an unparalleled Butter Chicken, attention to detail is key. Firstly, do not skimp on the marination time for the chicken; longer marination ensures tender, flavorful results. Secondly, blend the gravy until it is absolutely smooth, then strain it through a fine-mesh sieve. This extra step removes any fibrous bits from the tomatoes and cashews, resulting in the signature silky texture. Thirdly, always use high-quality, ripe tomatoes or good crushed canned tomatoes for the best flavor base. Finally, remember to add the fresh cream and finishing butter towards the end of the cooking process and avoid vigorous boiling thereafter, as this preserves their delicate flavor and prevents the gravy from splitting.

## Serving Suggestions

Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is best enjoyed with accompaniments that can soak up its rich, creamy gravy. Classic choices include warm, fluffy Naan bread, especially garlic naan or butter naan, which are perfect for scooping up every last drop. Alternatively, serve it with fragrant Basmati rice, such as jeera rice or plain steamed rice, allowing the subtle rice flavors to complement the robust curry. For a more elaborate meal, pair it with soft, layered Laccha Paratha or Tandoori Roti. A simple side salad of sliced onions, cucumbers, and tomatoes, or a cooling raita (yogurt dip), can provide a refreshing counterpoint to the richness of the Butter Chicken.

jsonLd.name: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani

Its harmonious blend of tangy, sweet, and subtly spicy flavors, finished with fresh cream and butter, makes it a beloved classic for a truly authentic Indian lunch.
jsonLd.keywords: Indian, Indian, authentic, traditional, lunch, Indian, Butter Chicken, authentic, traditional, lunch, Mughlai, Murgh Makhani
recipeIngredient: 500g Boneless Chicken Thighs or Breast (cut into 1-inch pieces)
2 Black Cardamom Pods (optional, for deeper flavor)
1 Large Onion (finely chopped (optional, for a sweeter base))
800g Canned Crushed Tomatoes (or 5-6 medium ripe tomatoes, blanched, peeled, and pureed)
1/4 cup Cashew Halves (soaked in warm water for 15 minutes)
